name: Silver Route - Castilblanco de los Arroyos Loop
name_original: El Ronquillo desde Burguillos – circular por Castilblanco de los Arroyos
description: This difficult 51.6-mile road cycling route from El Ronquillo offers panoramic views and varied terrain, including some unpaved sections.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Burguillos offer diverse terrain, characterized by rolling hills and varied landscapes. The region features quiet, scenic roads that traverse areas with olive groves and provide views of the surrounding countryside. Cyclists can expect routes with both gentle gradients and more challenging ascents, leading through rural tracks and past historic bridges. The area's topography includes elevated sections that provide panoramic views, making it suitable for different levels of road cycling experience. Best road cycling routes around Burguillos The most popular road cycling route is Silver Route - Castilblanco de los Arroyos Loop, a 51.6 miles (83.0 km) trail that takes 3 hours 56 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through varied terrain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Venta del Alto – Las Pajanosas loop from Castilblanco de los Arroyos, a difficult 63.9 miles (102.9 km) path. This route offers extensive mileage and considerable climbing, providing a challenging experience. Local road cyclists also love the Guillena – Guillena Town Hall loop from Castilblanco de los Arroyos, a 42.2 miles (68.0 km) trail leading through the local countryside, often completed in about 2 hours 56 minutes. Road cycling around Burguillos is defined by rolling hills, rural tracks, and scenic views, with routes that cater to both moderate and difficult ability levels. The network includes options for various fitness levels, from steady rides through olive groves to routes with sustained climbs. The routes in Burguillos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from 31 reviews. More than 1300 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Burguillos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Burguillos?
A: There are over 30 road cycling routes documented around Burguillos on komoot, offering diverse options for cyclists. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Burguillos?
A: Road cycling around Burguillos is characterized by rolling hills and varied landscapes. You'll find quiet, scenic roads that traverse areas with olive groves and provide views of the surrounding countryside. Routes include both gentle gradients and more challenging ascents, leading through rural tracks and past historic bridges, particularly in the broader region of Andalusia.

Q: What is the overall difficulty level of road cycling in Burguillos?
A: The routes in Burguillos cater to a range of abilities. Out of 31 routes, 3 are considered easy, 19 are moderate, and 9 are difficult. This variety ensures options for both casual riders and those seeking a significant challenge with considerable climbing.

Q: Are there scenic viewpoints or landmarks along the road cycling routes in Burguillos?
A: Yes, many routes offer scenic views. The region features elevated sections that provide panoramic vistas. You might encounter historic bridges, especially if cycling near areas like Burguillos de Toledo. For specific points of interest, consider exploring highlights such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2703682" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterway Path Bridge&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2703683" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gergal Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural features can I expect to see while cycling around Burguillos?
A: While cycling around Burguillos, you can expect to see extensive olive groves, rural tracks, and the general countryside of Andalusia. The broader region also features natural beauty like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5084729" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rivera de Huelva&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2703683" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gergal Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, offering serene environments and picturesque views.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Burguillos?
A: The best months for road cycling in Burguillos are typically spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summers can be very hot in Andalusia, making early mornings or late afternoons more suitable for rides. Winters are generally mild, but it's always advisable to check local weather forecasts.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling routes in Burguillos?
A: Yes, there are a few easier routes suitable for families or beginners. Out of the 31 available routes, 3 are classified as easy, offering less challenging terrain and elevation. These routes provide a good starting point for those looking for a more relaxed cycling experience.

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes in Burguillos?
A: Many of the road cycling routes around Burguillos are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994034638/silver-route-castilblanco-de-los-arroyos-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Silver Route - Castilblanco de los Arroyos Loop&lt;/a&gt; or the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/34004426" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guillena – Guillena Town Hall loop from Castilblanco de los Arroyos&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Burguillos?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from 31 reviews. Cyclists often praise the quiet, scenic roads, the diverse terrain with rolling hills and olive groves, and the variety of routes that cater to different fitness levels, from steady rides to sustained climbs.

Q: Are there options for long-distance road cycling in Burguillos?
A: Absolutely. Burguillos offers several routes for cyclists looking for longer distances. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5292111" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cazalla de la Sierra – Cuesta de Los Melonares loop from Castilblanco de los Arroyos&lt;/a&gt; covers over 134 kilometers with significant elevation gain, providing an extensive and challenging experience.

Q: What is the typical duration for road cycling routes in Burguillos?
A: Route durations vary significantly based on distance and difficulty. Shorter, moderate routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/34004426" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guillena – Guillena Town Hall loop from Castilblanco de los Arroyos&lt;/a&gt; can be completed in under 3 hours, while longer, difficult routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24546559" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Venta del Alto – Las Pajanosas loop from Castilblanco de los Arroyos&lt;/a&gt; can take over 4.5 hours.

Q: What is the road surface quality like for cycling in Burguillos?
A: The road surface quality generally varies. While many routes feature well-maintained roads, some sections may include rural tracks or older paths. In the broader region, some dirt tracks might be occasionally damaged by erosion, particularly on less frequented routes, but the main road cycling routes are typically suitable for road bikes.
tips: A small hermitage from the middle of the 16th century. The image of the Virgen de Gracia is also from the 16th century, 22 cm high and finished with polychrome.

The classic slope of ascent to the Cuesta de la Media Fanega among Seville cyclists. It is located on the old national road N-630, thanks to which the traffic is scarce. The ascent begins next to the recreational area, after the descent from Las Baterías, and ends at Venta del Alto.

What to see when you are in Guillena - the most important monuments: Parroquia de Ntra. Sra. de la Granada, Plaza de España, Plaza de Toros, Palacio de Parladé. Guillena is part of the Camino de Santiago (Vía de la Plata between Seville and Astorga).

faqs.question: How challenging is the road cycling route from El Ronquillo to Burguillos via Castilblanco de los Arroyos?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as a difficult road cycling route, requiring good to very good fitness. While primarily on paved surfaces, be prepared for some unpaved segments where you might need to push your bike. The varied terrain includes significant elevation changes, making it a demanding ride.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain and scenery can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: The route offers varied terrain, from paved roads to potentially unpaved sections. You'll cycle through picturesque Mediterranean forest landscapes, dehesas with palm hearts, retama, and holm oaks. Expect stunning views of the Gergal Reservoir and the Riviera de Huelva. Keep an eye out for the Cerro del Calvario Viewpoint, which offers panoramic vistas of valleys filled with orchards, olive groves, and meadows.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Key highlights along the route include the Hermitage of Our Lady of Grace, the challenging Cuesta de la Media Fanega, and passing through the settlement of Guillena. The route also offers scenic views of the Gergal Reservoir and the Riviera de Huelva, with several viewpoints along the way.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 83 kilometers long with over 1000 meters of elevation gain. For an average racebike cyclist, it typically takes around 4 hours of active cycling time to complete, not including stops.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas are not detailed, as a circular route starting from Burguillos, you would typically find parking within or on the outskirts of Burguillos or Castilblanco de los Arroyos. Look for public parking spaces in these towns to begin your ride.

faqs.question: Is public transport available to reach the starting point?
faqs.answer: Information on specific public transport links directly to the starting point in Burguillos for cyclists is not readily available. It is advisable to check local bus schedules for services connecting to Burguillos from larger nearby cities like Seville, keeping in mind bike transport policies.

faqs.question: Is this route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This is a road cycling route, and while dogs are generally not suitable companions for racebike cycling, if you were to consider walking sections, be aware that some parts of the route may pass through fields with loose cattle. If you bring a dog, it should always be on a leash, and you must be prepared to close any gates you open.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The region of Andalusia is popular for cycling. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for cycling. Summers can be very hot, making early morning rides essential, while winters are milder but can still be cool and wet. Check local weather forecasts before you go.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No specific permits or entrance fees are required to cycle this route. It utilizes public roads and paths. However, always respect private property and local regulations.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route does intersect with other paths. Notably, it covers sections of the "Mtb Vía de la Plata" and the "Vía de la Plata hiking - 01 Sevilla to Merida" for approximately 28% of its length. Smaller segments also overlap with the "Ruta del Agua" and "Camino de Castilblanco".

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay overnight along the route?
faqs.answer: The route passes through settlements like Guillena, Burguillos, and Castilblanco de los Arroyos. These towns typically offer cafes, restaurants, and potentially accommodation options for cyclists looking to refuel or break up their journey.
